user.title: PV History Forecast
user.description: The prefix defines the names of all sensors created (e.g. "pv_hist" → sensor.pv_hist_remaining_today / _min / _max / _tomorrow). The default Home Assistant SQLite database is used automatically.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure PV History Forecast

data.sensor_clouds: Cloud Coverage Sensor (Optional — leave empty: auto-sensor is kept, works from day 1)
data.sensor_uv: UV Index Sensor (Optional — leave empty: auto-sensor is kept)

sensors.title: Configure Sensors

data.sensor_clouds: Cloud Coverage Sensor (Optional — leave empty: auto-sensor is created automatically, works from day 1)
data.sensor_uv: UV Index Sensor (Optional — leave empty: auto-sensor is created automatically)
data.pv_history_days: Days of cloud history to use (only applies when a cloud coverage sensor is selected above; default: 30)

error.invalid_db_url: Could not access the Home Assistant recorder. Make sure the recorder integration is active.
error.sqlite_required: Home Assistant is not using a SQLite database. This integration only supports SQLite.
error.sensor_pv_required: PV panel energy sensor is required
error.must_be_weather_entity: Please select a weather entity from the list (weather.*)
error.must_be_sensor_entity: Please select a sensor from the list (sensor.*)
error.sensor_pv_wrong_unit: The PV sensor must use kWh or Wh as its unit (device class: energy)
error.no_cloud_forecast: The weather entity does not provide cloud_coverage data in its forecast. Without cloud data the PV forecast cannot work. Please choose a different weather provider.
error.no_forecast_support: The selected weather entity does not support the weather.get_forecasts action. Please select a different weather entity (e.g. from the built-in weather integration or OpenWeatherMap).
